name: ActiveCollab Project Management API
what_it_does: Project management platform with REST API — enabling agencies and freelancers to manage projects, tasks, and time tracking while integrating client invoicing and billing, with features for client-facing project portals, recurring tasks, estimates, workload management, and direct payment processing via Stripe.
use_cases: Managing projects, tasks, and subtasks via ActiveCollab REST API for workflow automation
Tracking time entries and generating invoices via ActiveCollab API for billing pipeline integration
Syncing ActiveCollab project data with accounting systems (QuickBooks, Xero) via API
Querying project status, time logs, and profitability metrics via ActiveCollab API for reporting
Managing team members, workload, and availability via ActiveCollab API for resource planning
not_for: Enterprise-scale project management with complex dependencies (use MS Project or Smartsheet)
Agile/Scrum teams with sprint-focused workflows (use Jira for full Scrum/Kanban support)
Pure task management without billing needs (use Asana or Todoist for simple task tracking)
best_when: A creative agency or professional services firm needs integrated project management and client billing — combining task management, time tracking, client portal, and invoice generation without switching between multiple tools.
avoid_when: You're a software development team (Jira is better), don't need invoicing, have enterprise PM requirements, or don't manage client-facing projects.

auth.notes: API key (token) from ActiveCollab account. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. Webhook for task, project, and invoice events. REST API v5 for all operations. PHP and Python SDKs available. Self-hosted option (own-your-data) alongside cloud. Integration with Slack, Zapier, and accounting tools. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.notes: Serbian company (Novi Sad). Founded 2007. Bootstrapped and profitable. Remote-first company. 50,000+ customers worldwide. Focus on agencies, creative teams, and professional services. Self-hosted option for data ownership — rare in modern SaaS. PHP and Python SDKs. Direct Stripe payment integration for client invoicing. Profitability reporting and job costing as differentiators. Strong in EU market given data residency options.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v5 for project, task, time, and invoice management. API key bearer auth. Good documentation with PHP and Python SDK examples. Webhook for workflow events. Rate limit ~60 req/min. Self-hosted option for data residency. Serbian company with GDPR compliance. Self-hosted requires own infrastructure management.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Project reads fully idempotent. Task reads idempotent. Time entry reads idempotent. Invoice reads idempotent by number. Project creation not idempotent — check by name. Time entry creation requires date and task ID for deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit of 60 req/min — batch operations carefully to avoid throttling
No OAuth2 — API key provides full account access without scope granularity
Self-hosted option requires separate infrastructure — API endpoints differ from cloud
Serbian company — verify EU data residency for cloud option
Billing integration (Stripe) is built-in — be careful with invoice and payment API calls

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. EU and US data residency (self-hosted for full control). API key auth. Serbian company with GDPR awareness. Self-hosted option eliminates cloud data residency concerns. Project data may include client confidential information — restrict API key access. Invoice and payment data requires careful handling. Stripe integration for payments — PCI compliance via Stripe.
